ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEFAN SOBCZAK'S MOTION TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE A PLAUSIBLE EXPLANATION ABOUT WHY LEAVE TO AMEND WAS NOT SOUGHT EARLIER. PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION THAT HE DID NOT LEARN ABOUT THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE PROPOSED CAUSES OF ACTION UNTIL HIS OWN DEPOSITION MAKES LITTLE SENSE. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT SUBMITS EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT, AT THE VERY LEAST, PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF THE FACTS IN DECEMBER 2014. (DECLARATION OF WALTER C. PFEFFER, EXS. Q-R.) DEFENDANT HAS ESTABLISHED THAT IT WILL BE UNDULY PREJUDICED BY THE DELAY, GIVEN THAT DISCOVERY HAS BEEN COMPLETED AND TRIAL IS SET TO COMMENCE IN APPROXIMATELY TWO MONTHS. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE RULES OF COURT. CRC 3.1324 SPECIFIES WHAT MUST BE INCLUDED IN A DECLARATION FILED IN SUPPORT OF A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DECLARATION OF FRANK P. SARRO DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ADDRESS ANY OF THE REQUIREMENTS SET FORTH IN CRC 3.1324. JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/SRB)
MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-21-2015 HELD AND SETTLED. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 610 ON NOV-03-2015 AT 10:30 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385. (8.0)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk MOlopernes; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-24-2015 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF STEFAN SOBCZAK'S Motion To File First Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff does not provide a plausible explanation about why leave to amend was not sought earlier. Plaintiff's contention that he did not learn about the facts giving rise to the proposed causes of action until his own deposition makes little sense. Additionally, Defendant submits evidence establishing that, at the very least, Plaintiff was aware of the facts in December 2014. (Declaration of Walter C. Pfeffer, Exs. Q-R.) Defendant has established that it will be unduly prejudiced by the delay, given that discovery has been completed and trial is set to commence in approximately two months. Further, Plaintiff's motion does not comply with the Rules of Court. CRC 3.1324 specifies what must be included in a declaration filed in support of a motion for leave to amend. The Declaration of Frank P. Sarro does not adequately address any of the requirements set forth in CRC 3.1324. Judge Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Set For Trial On Sep-21-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
